RESPONSES TO DEFTS OBJECTIONS TO PLTFS EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF THEIR MOTION FOR A PRELINMINARY INJUNCTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MCLAUGHLIN III, ANDREW C. AS TRUSTEE FOR THE ANDREW C. MCLAUGHLIN III REVOCABLE TRUST AND AS TRUSTEE FOR THE TRUST FBO DIANA M. LEBENZON AND AS TRUSTEE FOR THE TURST FBO JOAN QUIGLEY

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ANDREW MCLAUGHLIN'S APPLICATION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED, IN PART. PLAINTIFFS HAVE DEMONSTRATED A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON ITS CLAIM TO A PRESCRIPTIVE EASEMENT. PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF IRREPARABLE HARM IF DEFENDANT IS NOT ENJOINED. DEFENDANT, ITS AGENTS, TENANTS, CUSTOMERS AND EMPLOYEES, ARE PRELIMINARILY ENJOINED FROM SMOKING, LITTERING, PLACING & STORING PARTY MATERIALS, CONTRUCTING STRUCTURES, REMODELLING, USING SUTTER STREET ALLEY AS A WORKPLACE. BECAUSE THE PARTIES HAVE NOT ADDRESSED THE ISSUE OF AN APPROPRIATE UNDERTAKING, WHICH PLAINTIFFS ARE REQUIRED TO POST, THE COURT SETS THE UNDERTAKING AT $25,000. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Continued To Feb-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jul-31-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Miles Holden (415-777-3200) from Hason and Bridget appearing for Defedant and moving party / Atty. John Klotsche (916-491-3020) from Hason and Bridget appearing for Defendant and moving party/ Atty. Fred Blum (415-397-9006) appearing for Cross Defendant and opposing party./ Atty. Antonio Garcia Jr. (213-412-2661) appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

Argued - The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: DEFENDANT PALMER CITY-CORE STOCKTON STREET'S Motion For A Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Subject to the following conditions. 1. All garbage/grease recetacles maintained by Plaintiff and located in the paved enclosure, must be tightly closed at all times (except at the times of garbage/grease deposit). Receptacles shall not be left open and unattended at any times. 2. Within 2 weeks of notice of entry of order this order, Plaintiff must obtain additional receptacles to ensure that (a) at no time during the day or night any of the receptacles are overflowing, and (b) that ther are enough receptacles for all the lids to be shut not eithstanding the amount of refuse accumulated. Order signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Bautista, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christine Hiler, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett, Tel. 415.995.5102, for cross complainant and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANTS CYMAN PARTNERSHIP; ANDREW C. MCLAUGHLIN III, as Trustee for the Andrew C. McLaughlin III Revocable Trust; THOMAS O. MCLAUGHLIN, as Trustee for the Thomas O. McLaughlin SEPARATE PROPERTY TRUST; ANDREW C. MCLAUGHLIN III AND THOMAS O. MCLAUGHLIN as Trustees for the Trust FBO DIANA M. LEBENZON; ANDREW C MCLAUGHLIN III AND THOMAS O MCLAUGHLIN as Trustees for the Trust FBO JOAN QUIGLEY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled Defendant has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for declaratory relief of the express easements. As pled, an actual controversy exists over the 1928 McEnerney Recordation and the 1943 Deed over light and air and the additional rights. A demurrer attacks the entirety of a cause of action, and not only part of a cause of action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Fred M. Blum, Esq. of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum, Tel. 415.397.9006, for plaintiff and moving party;
Miles Holden, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett, Tel. 415.777.3200, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANDREW MCLAUGHLIN's Application For A Preliminary Injunction is granted, in part. Plaintiffs have demonstrated a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Defendant's evidence does not demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on its claim to a prescriptive easement. Plaintiffs have shown a likelihood of irreparable harm if Defendant is not enjoined. Defendant, its agents, tenants, customers and employees, are preliminarily enjoined from smoking, littering, placing & storing party materials, contructing structures, remodelling, using Sutter Street Alley as a workplace. Because the parties have not addressed the issue of an appropriate undertaking, which Plaintiffs are required to post, the Court sets the undertaking at $25,000. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
